text.title: Testing (Multi-Language) — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when testing is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Test B reads state Test A wrote.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Random order breaks; parallel breaks; -k subset breaks. Fix: Reset state per test; use fixtures with setup + teardown.
Root cause: Tests pass alone, fail together. Fix: Encapsulate state in fixtures with teardown.

antipattern.description: Unit lane includes a 5s DB roundtrip.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Feedback loop dies; developers stop running tests locally. Fix: Move to integration lane; tag accordingly.

antipattern.description: Cleanup swallows exceptions; teardown reports green even when it failed.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Resource leaks; flaky next test. Fix: Let cleanup exceptions propagate; fix the underlying issue.

antipattern.description: Snapshot tests for everything; updates committed without review.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Tests prove nothing; reviewer rubber-stamps snapshot diffs. Fix: Snapshots for stable rendered output only; specific assertions for logic.
